stations.name: Blood Forge Smelting Works
stations.description: The Pact's primary refinery. Raw ore from Iron Reach enters one end; weapons-grade alloys leave the other. The station runs three shifts and the heat never fully dissipates — veterans say you can taste copper in the air on every deck. The forges are the largest outside Sol, and they were built for one purpose.
stations.condition_text: Critical infrastructure failure. Emergency lighting only. Station barely functional.

stations.name: Crimson War Citadel
stations.description: Heart of Crimson military power. The Citadel is a fortress first and a station second — forge decks ring with hammer on alloy, fuel bunkers sit under three meters of armor plating, and Fleet Command issues tasking orders rather than posting requests. Civilians are tolerated. Complaining is not.
stations.condition_text: Multiple systems running on emergency reserves. Lights flicker in the corridors.

stations.name: Iron Reach Mining Colony
stations.description: A no-frills mining station bolted to the largest asteroid in the mineral fields. Ore convoys launch on a schedule measured in ticks, not hours. The mess hall serves protein paste and carbon dust in roughly equal proportions, and nobody complains — everyone here is working toward the same forge.
stations.condition_text: Critical infrastructure failure. Emergency lighting only. Station barely functional.

stations.name: Ironhearth Station
stations.description: The hearth the war machine comes home to. Behind the military docking protocols and weapons inspections, Ironhearth is the Pact's most human place — families live here, children are born here, and veterans retire here. The market stocks food and clothing alongside ammunition, and the cantina pours something that isn't technically regulation but nobody's been court-martialed for it yet.
stations.condition_text: Critical infrastructure failure. Emergency lighting only. Station barely functional.

stations.name: The Anvil Arsenal
stations.description: Final assembly for the Crimson Pact's weapons systems. Alloys from Blood Forge arrive as ingots and leave as turrets, railgun barrels, and armor plate. The station's forgemaster maintains a tradition: every weapon carries the serial number of the pilot who supplied its raw materials. A warrior's honor, stamped in steel.
stations.condition_text: Critical infrastructure failure. Emergency lighting only. Station barely functional.

stations.name: The Crucible Garrison
stations.description: Where warriors are forged. Every Crimson captain passes through the Crucible — weapons certification, ship qualification, and the combat trials that separate officers from the rest. The station doubles as a chokepoint checkpoint with its sister station at The Rampart. Nothing enters Krynn space without passing both.
stations.condition_text: Critical infrastructure failure. Emergency lighting only. Station barely functional.

stations.name: The Rampart Checkpoint
stations.description: The outermost wall of Crimson space. Every ship entering the Pact's territory passes through the Rampart's weapons envelope first, then the Crucible's. Together they form a two-layer chokepoint that has never been breached. The station's weapons point outward — always outward — with enough firepower to hold off a fleet. Nobody asks what fleet.
stations.condition_text: Critical infrastructure failure. Emergency lighting only. Station barely functional.

stations.name: Cargo Lanes Freight Depot
stations.description: The Federation's logistics backbone. Every major trade route converges here — shipments from Gold Run, exports from Factory Belt, and bulk transfers bound for Market Prime all pass through the Freight Depot's sorting systems. The station is ugly, efficient, and processes more tonnage per cycle than Haven's Grand Exchange.
stations.condition_text: Multiple systems running on emergency reserves. Lights flicker in the corridors.

stations.name: Factory Belt Manufacturing Hub
stations.description: Where the Federation turns raw materials into trade goods. The Manufacturing Hub runs automated production lines around the clock, converting ore and gas into components the galaxy needs. Observant traders notice that shipments sometimes arrive from Treasure Cache without appearing on any Federation manifest — a clerical oversight, surely.
stations.condition_text: Critical infrastructure failure. Emergency lighting only. Station barely functional.

stations.name: Gold Run Extraction Hub
stations.description: The Federation's breadbasket. Five planets, two belts, and a workforce that never stops producing. The Extraction Hub coordinates everything — ore shipments to Market Prime, food shipments to Trader's Rest, and ice shipments everywhere. The name Gold Run isn't aspirational. It's a statement of quarterly earnings.
stations.condition_text: Critical infrastructure failure. Emergency lighting only. Station barely functional.

stations.name: Grand Exchange Station
stations.description: Center of galactic commerce. The three-deck Grand Bazaar moves goods from every empire, but it's the Promenade that moves credits — restaurants serving Solarian rations, cantinas pouring Crimson bloodwine, and neural arcades powered by Voidborn matrices keep visitors spending long past when they planned to leave. The Collective takes its cut of everything.
stations.condition_text: Most systems operational, but some facilities show signs of deferred maintenance.

stations.name: Market Prime Exchange
stations.description: Haven's wholesale gateway. Bulk commodities change hands here before reaching the Grand Bazaar's retail floors. The Exchange runs twenty-four-hour auction cycles, and the trading floor never sleeps. A percentage of every transaction flows back to the Federation — the house always wins.
stations.condition_text: Critical infrastructure failure. Emergency lighting only. Station barely functional.

stations.name: The Levy Customs Station
stations.description: The Federation's toll booth. Every ship entering Nebula space through this lane pays inspection fees, tariff duties, and processing charges — the specific rates depending on what you're carrying and how much the inspectors feel like charging today. The station's scanners check what comes in, not what goes out. Nobody wonders why.
stations.condition_text: Critical infrastructure failure. Emergency lighting only. Station barely functional.

stations.name: Trader's Rest Resort Station
stations.description: Where the money goes to relax. The docking fees alone would fund a frontier outpost for a year, but the cantinas serve real food, the repair bays use genuine parts instead of salvage, and the neural arcades run Voidborn entertainment matrices. The Federation's merchant class lives here and commutes to Haven — because even traders need somewhere that isn't a trading floor.
stations.condition_text: Critical infrastructure failure. Emergency lighting only. Station barely functional.

stations.name: Treasure Cache Trading Post
stations.description: The Nebula Federation's furthest outpost, and it shows. Customs inspections are optional, manifests are filed on the honor system, and the dockmaster's main qualification is knowing when not to ask questions. Conveniently, the route from here to Factory Belt to Trader's Rest bypasses The Levy entirely — a geographic coincidence the Federation has never gotten around to correcting.
stations.condition_text: Critical infrastructure failure. Emergency lighting only. Station barely functional.

stations.name: Deep Range Outpost
stations.description: The Outer Rim's real secondary capital. Where expeditions are funded, outfitted, and launched into the unknown. The Outpost looks like Frontier's younger sibling — same jury-rigged aesthetic, same indifference to building codes, but the docking bay is bigger and the charts on the wall are more current. If Frontier is where Rimmers live, Deep Range is where they work.
stations.condition_text: Critical infrastructure failure. Emergency lighting only. Station barely functional.

stations.name: First Step Memorial Station
stations.description: Built from salvaged colony infrastructure by Rimmers who refused to let First Step stay dead. The station is small, functional, and deliberately maintained as both an outpost and a memorial. A plaque in the docking bay lists every colonist by name. The cause of death column reads the same for all of them: 'unknown.'
stations.condition_text: Critical infrastructure failure. Emergency lighting only. Station barely functional.

stations.name: Frontier Station
stations.description: Home of the wanderers. Three captured destroyers and a Nebula freighter welded into a mobile station the locals call 'the Patchwork.' Nothing matches, everything is jury-rigged, and the water on Decks 7 through 12 tastes like reactor coolant — but it flies, it fights, and the docking bay is open to anyone who doesn't start trouble.
stations.condition_text: Multiple systems running on emergency reserves. Lights flicker in the corridors.

stations.name: Last Light Station
stations.description: The last friendly port before the deep frontier. Four planets, a mineral belt, and a gas cloud make Last Light the best-supplied outer ring system in the Rim — and the chokepoint position covering one approach to Frontier space means there's always someone keeping watch. The station bar has a tradition: returning explorers pin a light to the wall. The wall is covered.
stations.condition_text: Critical infrastructure failure. Emergency lighting only. Station barely functional.

stations.name: Starfall Salvage Station
stations.description: Built around a captured asteroid in the belt's densest region. Starfall attracts prospectors chasing uranium and lithium — materials the core empires need and can't easily source. The station runs a brisk trade in salvage, raw ore, and information about what's deeper in the frontier. No questions asked, no manifests required.
stations.condition_text: Critical infrastructure failure. Emergency lighting only. Station barely functional.

stations.name: Unknown Edge Waystation
stations.description: The line between explored and uncharted. Outbound explorers stock up here. Inbound ones sell their survey data and swap stories. The Waystation doubles as a chokepoint checkpoint — not as formalized as the Crimson Pact's gatekeeping, more of a 'someone should probably notice what's coming in from deep space' arrangement.
stations.condition_text: Multiple systems running on emergency reserves. Lights flicker in the corridors.

stations.name: Void Gate Outpost
stations.description: Last stop before the void. Explorers resupply here before disappearing into lawless space, and the ones who come back sell whatever they found. The Outpost doesn't ask where you've been or what you're carrying. The docking bay accepts anyone — pirates, explorers, smugglers — because this far from Frontier, you take what company you can get.
stations.condition_text: Critical infrastructure failure. Emergency lighting only. Station barely functional.

stations.name: Alpha Centauri Colonial Station
stations.description: The oldest station outside Sol. Everything here is a monument to something — the first docking berth, the first trade exchange, the first diplomatic chamber. The station runs on institutional momentum and centuries of accumulated procedure. Services are comprehensive, if unhurried. Forms are required. Patience is expected.
stations.condition_text: Critical infrastructure failure. Emergency lighting only. Station barely functional.

stations.name: Confederacy Central Command
stations.description: The seat of Solarian government and commerce. Everything here is certified, warrantied, and documented in triplicate — from the precision drydocks to the quantum-entangled trade exchange. A self-sustaining biosphere spans three decks, providing real air and real food, because the Confederacy believes civilization requires both.
stations.condition_text: Multiple systems running on emergency reserves. Lights flicker in the corridors.

stations.name: Nova Terra Central
stations.description: The de facto economic capital of the Solarian Confederacy, and nobody in Sol will admit it. Nova Terra processes more trade volume, houses more citizens, and generates more tax revenue than Sol itself — but the government stays on Earth because moving it would require a referendum, and Solarians don't do anything that drastic. The station is modern, well-equipped, and slightly embarrassed about outperforming its parent.
stations.condition_text: Critical infrastructure failure. Emergency lighting only. Station barely functional.

stations.name: Procyon Colonial Station
stations.description: A functioning station that's been 'temporarily underfunded' for longer than most pilots have been alive. The repair bay works but uses salvaged parts. The market is stocked but the selection is limited. The refueling depot charges Confederacy rates for fuel that arrives on a schedule best described as 'eventually.' Procyon has the resources and the location to be a major system. It just needs someone to invest in it.
stations.condition_text: Most systems operational, but some facilities show signs of deferred maintenance.

stations.name: Sirius Observatory Station
stations.description: The Confederacy's research hub. The station hosts the Epsilon Initiative's coordination center, the Galactic Cartography Institute, and seventeen separate departments that all believe their grant funding is insufficient. The cantina doubles as an academic conference venue, and arguments about stellar formation models get louder than combat alerts.
stations.condition_text: Critical infrastructure failure. Emergency lighting only. Station barely functional.

stations.name: Central Nexus
stations.description: Heart of Voidborn civilization. The station draws power from dimensional fractures, trades through probability fields, and stores cargo in pocket dimensions. Organic visitors are accommodated, though the air tastes faintly of ozone and mathematics, and the repair nanites sometimes return your ship subtly different.
stations.condition_text: Multiple systems running on emergency reserves. Lights flicker in the corridors.

stations.name: Node Alpha Processing Station
stations.description: The Collective's primary backup node. If Nexus Prime goes dark, Node Alpha assumes primary network coordination within three ticks. The station processes more data per cycle than the entire Solarian research network combined, and it's considered a secondary facility. Organic visitors are accommodated. The station's efficiency makes most of them uncomfortable.
stations.condition_text: Critical infrastructure failure. Emergency lighting only. Station barely functional.

stations.name: Node Beta Industrial Station
stations.description: The Collective's material conversion hub. Raw resources enter; infrastructure components leave. Node Beta's fabrication arrays produce with a precision that makes Crimson forge masters envious and Solarian engineers write papers. The station also hosts the deep-space listening post where the Signal was first detected — though that equipment has since been relocated to a more specialized facility.
stations.condition_text: Critical infrastructure failure. Emergency lighting only. Station barely functional.

stations.name: Node Gamma Relay Station
stations.description: Mid-ring relay node and the system where the Signal was isolated most clearly. The station's primary function is network coordination, but since the Signal discovery, a significant portion of its processing capacity has been redirected to analysis. Signal Master Threx maintains a permanent presence here. Visitors report that the station feels like it's listening.
stations.condition_text: Critical infrastructure failure. Emergency lighting only. Station barely functional.

stations.name: Synchrony Hub
stations.description: Where the Collective thinks together. Synchrony Hub coordinates the alignment of distributed consciousness across Voidborn space — a process that organic visitors can observe but not truly comprehend. The station has no industry, no mining infrastructure, and no resource extraction. It exists purely for thought. The atmosphere tastes of ozone and something organic visitors can't name.
stations.condition_text: Critical infrastructure failure. Emergency lighting only. Station barely functional.

stations.name: The Experiment Research Station
stations.description: The Collective's most remote and most classified facility. Positioned three jumps from Nexus Prime in a system with a pre-existing anomaly the Voidborn have never explained, the Research Station processes data that arrives encrypted and leaves the same way. Organic visitors are permitted but monitored. The station's purpose is officially 'fundamental research.' Nobody believes that's the whole story.
stations.condition_text: All systems operating at peak efficiency. The station hums with well-maintained infrastructure.
